title: T-035 — Workflow-as-data state-machine executor (4th rule_type) for WO/TO/release-to-warehouse flows
prompt:
# T-035 — Workflow-as-data state-machine executor (4th rule_type) for WO/TO/release-to-warehouse flows

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §7
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Extend the rule engine with a dedicated workflow-as-data evaluator: load a workflow definition (states, transitions, guards) from Reference.Rules where rule_type='workflow' and evaluate a transition request against current state. T-018 only stubbed the 'workflow' branch with a throw — this task implements it.

## Implementation contract
(1) Add packages/rule-engine/src/workflow.ts exporting evaluateTransition(workflowDef, currentState, requestedTransition, context) → { allowed: boolean, nextState: string | null, guard_failed: string[] | null, actions: Action[] }. (2) Workflow JSON shape: { rule_id, rule_type='workflow', states: string[], initial_state, transitions: [{from, to, trigger, guards: Condition[], actions: Action[]}] }. (3) Wire into executor.ts (T-018) so that rule_type='workflow' dispatches to evaluateTransition instead of throwing. (4) Honour dry-run mode (no side-effects). (5) Vitest replicates a sample WO state machine (DRAFT → READY → IN_PROGRESS → DONE) and asserts an illegal transition (DRAFT → DONE) returns allowed=false with guard_failed populated.

## Files (scope)
- packages/rule-engine/src/workflow.ts [create]
- packages/rule-engine/src/executor.ts [modify]
- packages/rule-engine/src/__tests__/workflow.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a §7 workflow rule with states ['DRAFT','READY','IN_PROGRESS','DONE'] and a DRAFT→READY transition with guard 'has_bom=true', when evaluateTransition runs with currentState='DRAFT' + context.has_bom=true, then allowed=true and nextState='READY'
2. Given the same workflow definition, when evaluateTransition is asked for DRAFT→DONE (no transition declared), then allowed=false, nextState=null, and guard_failed=['no_matching_transition']
3. Given dry-run=true, when evaluateTransition runs and the transition would emit outbox actions, then no rows are inserted into outbox_events and the actions are returned in the result for caller inspection
4. Given the executor is invoked with rule_type='workflow', when the workflow branch dispatches to evaluateTransition, then it no longer throws the 'unknown rule_type' error from T-018

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/rule-engine/src/__tests__/workflow.test.ts covering all four ACs before workflow.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/rule-engine test'

## Risk red lines
- Dry-run MUST be side-effect-free — same contract as T-018 §7
- Do not hard-code WO state names — they come from the workflow JSON, not from code
- Do not invent new rule_type values — workflow is the 4th and final type per PRD §7 table

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-018

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Implement the 4th rule_type 'workflow' (state-machine evaluator) and wire it into the executor stub from T-018.
pipeline_inputs.details: T-018 stubbed all 4 rule_types but only 3 work end-to-end ('cascading','conditional_required','gate'). The 'workflow' branch throws. This task implements evaluateTransition() honouring transitions[from,to,trigger,guards,actions], dispatches it from the executor when rule_type='workflow', and respects dry-run.
